executionHistory.summary: Created analyst-owned competitive parity feature spec covering adoption friction, interoperability, trust signals, and competitive gaps versus Claude Code, Roo Code, Cursor, and Cline.
Implemented Scope 01 capability truth and freshness hygiene with the new capability ledger, generated competitive docs, issue status blocks, and validation selftests.

Re-ran the live Scope 02 release-manifest, install-provenance, trust-doctor, framework-validate, release-check, and artifact-lint surfaces and refreshed test evidence without changing certification state.

Resolved Scope 02 stabilize finding ST-02-1 by adding explicit release-manifest schema validation to downstream trust readers, removing upgrade preview fallback arrays, refreshing the release manifest, and rerunning trust plus release validation surfaces.

Retested Scope 02 after ST-02-1 by rerunning release-manifest, install-provenance, trust-doctor, framework-validate, release-check, artifact-lint, and live-test hygiene scans; all required trust surfaces stayed green and certification state was left unchanged.

Refreshed the Scope 02 post-repair test packet by rerunning the live release-manifest, framework-validate, and release-check baseline; the trust chain was green again and the trust-doctor malformed-manifest path stayed free of shell trap errors.

Implemented Scope 04 maturity-tier governance profiles across the adoption registry, CLI profile surfaces, repo-local policy state, readiness messaging, scope-owned docs, and a new profile-transition selftest. Live Scope 04 profile behavior is green, and the feature remains in progress only because later scopes are still open and certification-owned completion did not advance.

Closed Scope 05 with scope-bounded rollback and change-boundary proof, and implemented Scope 06 supported interop apply, migration guidance, generated migration matrix, selftests, and broader framework regression coverage without changing validate-owned certification state.

Refreshed the stale generated competitive docs and `bubbles/release-manifest.json`, recorded implement-owned refresh evidence in the feature packet, and left validate-owned certification state unchanged.

Ran current-session regression verification for the parent packet: framework validation and release-check both passed, traceability stayed intact at 54 scenario mappings, and the regression baseline guard found no peer-spec reruns or route collisions to remediate.

Re-verified the existing Scope 06 simplify cleanup from authoritative source-repo surfaces, replaced the stale installed-copy artifact-lint blocker with the passing source-repo packet lint result, and recorded simplify-owned execution provenance without changing validate-owned certification state.

Closed the routed stabilize finding by restoring executable bits on 13 bubbles/scripts shell surfaces, rerunning doctor plus release-check from the source repo, and refreshing implement-owned packet evidence without changing validate-owned certification state.

Re-ran artifact lint, doctor, and release-check after the executable-bit repair; the earlier stabilize-owned operational finding is now closed and no new stability defects were detected in the current session.

Re-ran the repaired packet's security phase with current-session artifact lint, packet scan, interop import/apply, runtime-lease, release-manifest, framework-validate, and release-check evidence; no open security findings remained and the execution phase was recorded without changing validate-owned certification state.

Re-verified the competitive parity docs phase from authoritative source-repo surfaces, confirmed packet lint plus docs selftests and release-check are green, reconciled the stale report blocker narrative against the actual CLI, installer, registry, and manifest surfaces, and recorded docs-owned execution provenance without changing validate-owned certification state.

Reconciled the active design against the shipped release-manifest, install-provenance, profile, and supported interop-apply surfaces, confirmed spec-design coherence at a high level, and recorded design-owner provenance without changing validate-owned certification state.

Reviewed packet coherence for the product-to-delivery pass, confirmed the six active scopes remain aligned with current spec and design, verified via artifact lint that no planning-owned DoD evidence gap remains in scopes.md, identified the stale blocker language in report.md as foreign-owned report content, and recorded planning provenance without changing validate-owned certification state.

Independently reran the parent packet's state-transition guard, artifact lint, framework validation, and release-check from the source repo, verified no new audit findings beyond the open validate and chaos phase-record closure, and recorded audit-owned evidence without changing validate-owned certification state.

Ran a bounded mixed-order chaos pass against the live source-repo maintainer surfaces using authoritative CLI and selftest commands, including runtime lease, docs parity, interop intake, repo-readiness, framework validation, and release-check; no new runtime or workflow regressions were found and no mutable fixtures or downstream state required cleanup.

Validation reran packet lint, traceability, implementation reality, framework validation, release-check, artifact freshness, and the state-transition guard from the authoritative source-repo surfaces; the only blocking gate was the missing validate phase record, which is now certified and promoted to done.

scopeProgress.name: Release Manifest And Install Provenance Trust
Supported Interop Apply And Evaluator Migration Guidance

notes: Validate-owned certification was granted on 2026-04-06 after authoritative packet lint, traceability, framework validation, release-check, artifact freshness, and state-transition checks confirmed the only blocking gate was the missing validate phase record.
